coaching.title: Easy Hilly Run — Peak Week
coaching.message: 9 miles easy on hilly terrain, strict Zone 2 (~124-138 bpm). This is the hardest week of the plan, so 'easy' is the assignment — keep effort conversational even on the climbs and power-hike anything that spikes your HR. In Buenos Aires, the Bosques de Palermo loops and the Reserva Ecológica give you rolling ground; if it's flat, add relaxed surges on any overpass or bridge to nod at the Julian Alps' 2,500m of vert. Nose-breathing pace all the way.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Under ~75 min you can run this fasted or on a light 30-40g carb pre-run (banana, medialuna in a pinch). If you extend past 75 min, take 60g carbs/hr and treat it as gut training for race day. Rehydrate with electrolytes afterward — Buenos Aires winter is dry — and hit 25-30g protein plus real carbs within 45 min to bank recovery for the heavy week.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Peak week is won by what you do between runs. Keep this run genuinely easy so it's recovery, not another stressor — 10 min of legs-up-the-wall and calf/quad mobility tonight, and protect 8+ hours of sleep. Extra sleep, extra food, extra focus: that's the week's motto.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: This is the biggest week of a 22-week build and you're 62 days out — the fitness is being forged right now. Holding back on an easy day when your legs feel good is a discipline that pays off at kilometer 40 in Kranjska Gora. Trust the plan, run the effort you're told, and let the adaptation do its quiet work.
